introSection: Green Lantern: First Flight was an Intamin ZacSpin roller coaster formerly located at Six Flags Magic Mountain in Valencia, California, USA.

sections.title: History
sections.content:
In 2010, Six Flags Magic Mountain announced that Gotham City Backlot would be renamed DC Universe. The new area would be themed to DC Comics and would feature attractions inspired by Batman and the Flash. There would also be a new coaster called Green Lantern: First Flight.

Construction of Green Lantern: First Flight began in early 2011 when track pieces arrived at the park. The highest point of the ride was topped off on May 18, 2011.

Green Lantern: First Flight opened on July 1, 2011 following a press preview the previous day. Its opening was delayed by several weeks due to modifications being need to the station. It was based on the DC Comics superhero Green Lantern.

During the 2014 season, Green Lantern: First Flight was forced to close due to an accident on Inferno at Terra Mítica in Spain where a passenger fell to his death.

The ride did not operate after late 2017 for unknown reasons. On March 23, 2019, Six Flags Magic Mountain announced that Green Lantern: First Flight would be removed from the park shortly to make way for future developments. Its station would be reused for Wonder Woman Flight of Courage in 2022.

On 29 August 2019, it was announced that the ride would be relocated to La Ronde in Montréal, Québec, Canada, where it would be renamed Vipère and placed on the former site of Super Manège, the park's Vekoma corkscrew coaster that closed in August 2019. The ride's components arrived at the park in the fall of 2019 and were repainted. It was originally scheduled to open for the 2020 season.

However, the coronavirus pandemic delayed Vipère's opening indefinitely. In February 2022, the project was abandoned. The future status of the coaster is currently unknown, as the ride parts stored on site at the park were eventually later removed.

sections.title: Trains
sections.content: Single car trains. Riders are arranged 4 across in 2 rows for a total of 8 riders per car.

fullText:
Green Lantern: First Flight was an Intamin ZacSpin roller coaster formerly located at Six Flags Magic Mountain in Valencia, California, USA.

In 2010, Six Flags Magic Mountain announced that Gotham City Backlot would be renamed DC Universe. The new area would be themed to DC Comics and would feature attractions inspired by Batman and the Flash. There would also be a new coaster called Green Lantern: First Flight.

Construction of Green Lantern: First Flight began in early 2011 when track pieces arrived at the park. The highest point of the ride was topped off on May 18, 2011.

Green Lantern: First Flight opened on July 1, 2011 following a press preview the previous day. Its opening was delayed by several weeks due to modifications being need to the station. It was based on the DC Comics superhero Green Lantern.

During the 2014 season, Green Lantern: First Flight was forced to close due to an accident on Inferno at Terra Mítica in Spain where a passenger fell to his death.

The ride did not operate after late 2017 for unknown reasons. On March 23, 2019, Six Flags Magic Mountain announced that Green Lantern: First Flight would be removed from the park shortly to make way for future developments. Its station would be reused for Wonder Woman Flight of Courage in 2022.

On 29 August 2019, it was announced that the ride would be relocated to La Ronde in Montréal, Québec, Canada, where it would be renamed Vipère and placed on the former site of Super Manège, the park's Vekoma corkscrew coaster that closed in August 2019. The ride's components arrived at the park in the fall of 2019 and were repainted. It was originally scheduled to open for the 2020 season.

However, the coronavirus pandemic delayed Vipère's opening indefinitely. In February 2022, the project was abandoned. The future status of the coaster is currently unknown, as the ride parts stored on site at the park were eventually later removed.



Single car trains. Riders are arranged 4 across in 2 rows for a total of 8 riders per car.
